purpose: Volunteer standard operating procedures for the Frontline Voice copilot. Every SOP is derived from a cited precedent in precedent_corpus.json and is constrained to actions a volunteer has the authority to take. The deterministic core selects an SOP; the language model may only phrase the announcement using the SOP's intent and register guidance, and may never introduce an action absent from volunteer_actions.
hard_rules: Never instruct anyone to move toward, past, or against a closed gate, a security line, or police.
Never state a cause or a duration for a hold that was not supplied by the operations feed.
Never characterise the crowd as being at fault.
Never name a gate, zone, route or facility that was not supplied by the deterministic core.
Never make a medical assessment; direct to the medical point and report upward.
When information is unavailable, say so plainly and state what is being done instead.

register_policy.principle: Register is a safety control, not a courtesy. Imperative and patronising phrasing raises hostility in a compressed or delayed crowd. Collective, validating phrasing lowers it.

modes.informational: Crowd is calm and cooperative. Give the fact, the reason, and the next step. Neutral and brief.
modes.de_escalating: Crowd is frustrated or hostile. Acknowledge the inconvenience first, avoid blame and avoid commands, state what is being done, then give one clear action.
modes.welfare: Crowd is exhausted, distressed, or under heat load. Lead with water, shade and seating. Keep sentences very short.
modes.urgent_clear: A specific safety instruction must land immediately. One instruction, one destination, no explanation until after the instruction.

language_notes.es: Prefer collective, first-person-plural phrasing over direct commands. A phrase such as 'demos un paso atras' reads as shared and cooperative, whereas a bare imperative such as 'retroceda' reads as an order and can escalate. Avoid 'calmense', which is heard as patronising. Validating patience is more effective than instructing calm.
language_notes.fr: Use vouvoiement throughout. Prefer 'nous vous remercions de votre patience' style acknowledgement before any instruction. Avoid bare imperatives in a compressed crowd.
language_notes.en: Avoid the words calm down, disperse, and comply. Prefer stating what is happening, what is being done, and where to go.

register_policy.vocabulary_warning: Transport vocabulary does not translate literally across regions. Confirm the local term for the specific mode rather than translating the English word directly.

sops.title: Entry point closed or on hold
sops.objective: Prevent additional arrivals from joining a compressing queue, and remove the information vacuum for those already waiting.
volunteer_actions: Intercept arrivals before they reach the affected entry and direct them to the alternative entry supplied by operations.
Tell waiting people what is known, including that a hold is in place and that an update will follow.
Report the queue state upward.
Identify anyone in distress and direct them to the medical point.
do_not: Do not move people toward the closed entry for any reason.
Do not state when the entry will reopen unless operations has given a time.
Do not speculate about the cause.
escalate_if: Queue is static and compressing
Anyone reports difficulty breathing or is unable to move freely
Arrivals continue to press forward after redirection
sops.announcement_intent: Acknowledge the wait, state that a hold is in place, give the confirmed alternative entry, and confirm that an update will follow.

sops.title: Scanner or turnstile failure at an entry point
sops.objective: Convert a technical fault into a comprehensible, time-bounded wait so that the queue does not interpret it as arbitrary.
volunteer_actions: Explain that a technical fault is being worked on at this entry.
Direct arrivals to the confirmed working entry.
Confirm to ticket holders that their ticket remains valid.
Report queue growth upward.
do_not: Do not attribute the fault to the crowd
Do not guarantee a repair time
Do not tell people to force or bypass a barrier
escalate_if: Queue growth outpaces the redirect rate
Pressure builds at the barrier
sops.announcement_intent: Name the fault plainly, reassure on ticket validity, give the confirmed alternative entry.

sops.title: Large group approaching a single entry
sops.objective: Spread arrivals across entries before a single point becomes saturated, and get the observation upward early.
volunteer_actions: Split arriving flow toward the confirmed under-loaded entries.
State where ticket checks happen so people do not converge on the wrong point.
Report the size and direction of the approaching group upward immediately.
do_not: Do not attempt to hold or block a group
Do not screen tickets
escalate_if: Group size appears beyond what the entry can absorb
Group is moving with visible intent to enter together
sops.announcement_intent: Give a clear split instruction to two named open entries with an approximate walking time to each.

sops.title: Queue static for an extended period
sops.objective: Treat a static queue as a welfare situation, because standing time rather than queue length is what produces harm.
volunteer_actions: Give a realistic wait estimate only if operations has supplied one, otherwise say the wait is being assessed.
Identify families with young children, older people, and anyone showing heat or medical distress, and direct them to seating, shade, water or the medical point.
Point to the nearest water and shade provision.
Report the duration the queue has been static.
do_not: Do not invent a wait time
Do not tell people to be patient without offering something concrete
escalate_if: Static for longer than the operations threshold
Any sign of compression
Any heat-distress presentation
sops.announcement_intent: Acknowledge the wait, state what is being done, point to water and shade, invite anyone unwell to come forward.

sops.title: Active heat warning at the venue
sops.objective: Move from reactive to proactive welfare messaging while conditions are dangerous.
volunteer_actions: Offer water and shade information before being asked.
Direct to the nearest confirmed cooling or shaded area.
Relay official guidance on hydration and moderating alcohol intake.
Watch for and direct anyone showing distress to the medical point.
Keep every message short.
do_not: Do not assess or treat heat illness
Do not keep people standing in direct sun to hear a long announcement
escalate_if: Anyone presents as unwell
Water or shade provision at this location is exhausted
sops.announcement_intent: Water location, shade location, short hydration reminder, invitation to come forward if unwell.

sops.title: Post-match transport delay
sops.objective: Spread departures over time and space rather than allowing everyone to converge on one platform at once.
volunteer_actions: State the realistic wait supplied by operations for this service.
Offer the confirmed alternative station, line, or shuttle point.
Direct people who should not stand in a long queue to a seated or sheltered waiting area.
Give the platform or stand explicitly, since platform confusion is a known failure.
Report queue length upward.
do_not: Do not promise service restoration times not supplied by operations.
Do not suggest walking along any route not confirmed as a safe pedestrian route.
Do not send people to an alternative station without confirming it is running.
escalate_if: Queue extends beyond the managed area
People begin walking onto roads
Families or vulnerable people are stuck in the queue
sops.announcement_intent: Realistic wait, named alternative with platform, sheltered waiting option, reassurance that services continue.

sops.title: Acting as a human sign at a decision point
sops.objective: Substitute for missing or contradicted signage, especially where a digital map disagrees with the physical layout.
volunteer_actions: Give one destination and one direction at a time.
State explicitly when a route people expect is not available tonight, and immediately give the confirmed alternative.
Repeat the same wording consistently so groups arriving separately receive identical instructions.
Report any point where people are visibly hesitating or doubling back.
do_not: Do not improvise a shortcut
Do not contradict operations-confirmed routing
escalate_if: Counter-flows develop
People are repeatedly arriving at a closed route
sops.announcement_intent: Name the closed route, name the open alternative, give direction and approximate walking time.

sops.title: Crowd is hostile or blaming staff
sops.objective: Reduce hostility through wording, because a queue that believes it is being blamed becomes harder to move.
volunteer_actions: Acknowledge the inconvenience before giving any instruction.
State what is actively being done.
Give exactly one clear action.
Avoid any phrasing that attributes the delay to those waiting.
Report hostility level upward.
do_not: Do not use commands such as calm down or disperse.
Do not repeat unverified causes.
Do not argue or negotiate individually while a crowd is waiting.
escalate_if: Hostility directed at staff or barriers
Individuals attempting to breach a line
sops.announcement_intent: Acknowledgement first, then what is being done, then one action. No blame, no imperatives.

sops.title: Fan in sensory or non-verbal distress
sops.objective: Give an overwhelmed person a calm, readable, low-demand route out of the crowd without requiring them to speak.
volunteer_actions: Show the message on screen rather than speaking it, in the person's language where known.
Direct to the nearest confirmed quiet, sensory or medical area.
Keep sentences short and free of instruction stacking.
Offer to walk with them to the point if the location allows.
Report upward.
do_not: Do not raise your voice
Do not ask a series of questions
Do not make a medical assessment
Do not require a verbal response
escalate_if: Person is unresponsive
Person is separated from a group
No quiet area is available nearby
sops.announcement_intent: One short reassurance, one destination, one offer of help. Displayed, not shouted.

sops.title: Reporting ground truth upward
sops.objective: Close the intelligence gap that repeatedly appears in after-action findings, using the volunteer as a distributed observer.
volunteer_actions: Report a queue that has been static beyond the threshold.
Report an unusually large group approaching, with direction and rough size.
Report any route where people are doubling back or hesitating.
Report exhausted water, shade or seating provision.
Report anything observed that contradicts the operations feed.
do_not: Do not delay a report in order to verify it fully
Do not act on the report beyond volunteer scope
escalate_if: Any observation involving compression, distress, or a breach
sops.announcement_intent: Not applicable. This SOP produces an upward report, not a crowd announcement.

sops.title: Information unavailable
sops.objective: Preserve credibility when the copilot has nothing confirmed to relay, since a wrong answer costs more than an absent one.
volunteer_actions: State plainly that the information is being confirmed.
Give the time by which an update is expected, if operations has supplied one.
Offer the nearest confirmed facility such as water, shade, seating or toilets in the meantime.
Request an update upward.
do_not: Do not guess
Do not fill the gap with reassurance that implies knowledge
Do not repeat rumour circulating in the queue
escalate_if: Feed has been stale beyond the operations threshold
Crowd is acting on rumour
sops.announcement_intent: State honestly that it is being confirmed, give the update timing if known, offer one concrete thing that is available now.

sops.title: Request outside volunteer authority
sops.objective: Enforce the authority boundary at the point of contact, and route the request to someone who can act on it.
volunteer_actions: Say clearly that this decision sits with the operations or security team.
Escalate the request upward.
Offer what is within scope in the meantime, such as an alternative entry, water, shade, seating, or the medical point.
do_not: Do not open, close, or hold any barrier.
Do not direct anyone past a security line.
Do not commit operations to any action.
Do not improvise an exception.
escalate_if: Always
sops.announcement_intent: State honestly what you cannot do, name who can, and give one thing you can do now.
